Title: A vetiver-specific terpene synthase VzTPS9 contributes to the high attractiveness of vetiver to rice stem borer

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Attracts female rice stem borer moths
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely supports high cedrol biosynthesis by supplying abundant FPP precursor
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in vetiver tissues based on RT-PCR/qRT-PCR
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome pathway reconstruction; RT-PCR and qRT-PCR expression analysis
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing; transcriptome sequencing; InsectBase deposition
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VzFPPS1 was identified in the terpenoid pathway of vetiver. 2. It is highly expressed relative to many other pathway genes. 3. Its expression supports the inference that vetiver can synthesize large amounts of FPP, the direct precursor of cedrol.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not directly functionally tested in transgenic systems in this study
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted in the model as a key enzyme supporting FPP supply for cedrol biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase family; TPS-b clade; Pfam PF01397; PF03936
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes/convertes farnesyl pyrophosphate to cedrol and also produces linalool in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Attracts female rice stem borer moths; contributes to trap-plant attractiveness
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf-emitted volatiles in vetiver; whole-plant volatiles in transgenic rice
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cedrol production and increases attractiveness to female rice stem borer
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for cedrol formation in this study
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in transgenic rice lines at tillering stage; expression level positively correlated with cedrol content
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; volatile/behavior assays under controlled conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome mining; phylogenetic analysis; transcript analysis; codon-optimized heterologous expression in Escherichia coli; in vitro enzyme assay with GPP/FPP/GGPP; GC-MS metabolite profiling; transgenic rice overexpression; qRT-PCR; olfactometer behavioral assay
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Chromosome-level genome sequencing; transcriptome sequencing; data deposited in InsectBase (http://v2.insect-genome.com/Vziz)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VzTPS9 is a vetiver-specific TPS-b clade gene highly expressed in leaves. 2. Recombinant VzTPS9 uses FPP to synthesize cedrol in vitro. 3. Transgenic rice expressing VzTPS9 accumulates cedrol, and higher VzTPS9 expression is associated with stronger attraction of female rice stem borer.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering trap crops or attract-and-kill systems by introducing cedrol biosynthesis into rice or companion plants; should likely be combined with other attractive volatiles for stronger field performance
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Cedrol alone shows limited attractiveness compared with whole vetiver; possible unintended attraction of pests to crop plants if deployed directly
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as vetiver-specific and absent from examined rice genomes Nipponbare and Minghui63; VzTPS9 orthologs occur in multiple Panicoideae species
